macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: IMF Downgrades Global Growth to 3.1%, Warns of Inflation Surge to 4.4% Amid Energy Shocks
items.summary: The IMF's World Economic Outlook reports global growth falling to 3.1% this year with headline inflation rising to 4.4%, driven by sharp increases in oil, gas, and commodity prices. Adverse scenarios predict even steeper declines to 2.5% growth and 5.4% inflation if disruptions persist.

items.title: Trump Threatens to Fire Fed Chair Powell If He Does Not Step Down
items.summary: President Trump has threatened to dismiss Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell unless he resigns, amid ongoing tensions over monetary policy. The story has garnered widespread coverage across political spectrums.

items.title: UN Warns of Economic Collapse in Yemen as Middle East Conflict Escalates
items.summary: UN Special Envoy Hans Grundberg alerts the Security Council to severe economic fallout in Yemen from rising fuel and food prices, disrupted imports, and regional tensions involving U.S., Israeli strikes on Iran and Houthi attacks. A fragile Iran-U.S. truce adds uncertainty.

items.title: US Producer Price Index Rises 0.5% in March, Goods Up 1.6%
items.summary: The Bureau of Labor Statistics reports the PPI for final demand advanced 0.5% in March, with goods prices surging 1.6% and services unchanged; annual increase stands at 4.0%. This follows import prices up 0.8% and export prices up 1.6%.

items.title: US Treasury Secretary Bessent Pushes IMF Reforms for Energy Abundance and Fiscal Prudence
items.summary: In an IMFC statement, Secretary Bessent highlights President Trump’s America First agenda for growth, energy abundance, and deregulation, urging IMF to focus on temporary lending, structural reforms, and private sector-led growth amid constrained financing.

items.title: EU Orders Meta to Roll Back WhatsApp AI Fee Over Antitrust Breach
items.summary: The European Union warns that Meta's WhatsApp AI fee violates antitrust rules and mandates a rollback. The decision underscores ongoing regulatory scrutiny of Big Tech pricing practices.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Crypto Market Faces $257M in Liquidations Over 24 Hours, Longs Hit Hardest
items.summary: The cryptocurrency market saw $257 million in liquidations in the past 24 hours, with $163 million from long positions and $93.82 million from shorts. Bitcoin and Ethereum led with $55.59 million and $43.59 million liquidated respectively, affecting 129,536 traders.

items.title: Bitcoin Surges 5% to $75,000 Amid Hopes of U.S.-Iran Peace Progress
items.summary: Bitcoin rose 5% over the past 24 hours to around $75,000, while Ethereum jumped 7% to nearly $2,400, hitting a two-month high. Markets are betting on de-escalation in U.S.-Iran tensions boosting sentiment.

items.title: Bitcoin Hits $76,000 High Before Pulling Back to $74,500 on Softer U.S. PPI Data
items.summary: Bitcoin reached a one-month high of $76,000 before easing to $74,500, supported by softer U.S. Producer Price Index data and whale accumulation. Profit-booking capped the rally amid ongoing volatility.

items.title: Crypto Market Rallies with $1.1 Billion ETP Inflows, Bitcoin Nears $75,000
items.summary: The crypto market rallied as Bitcoin approached $75,000 and Ethereum held above $2,300, driven by $1.1 billion in crypto exchange-traded product inflows. Overall market capitalization rose to $2.52 trillion amid institutional buying.

items.title: Over $152M in Long Positions Liquidated as Bitcoin Rally Stalls Below $76,000
items.summary: More than $152 million in long positions were liquidated in the past 24 hours, with Bitcoin accounting for over $50 million. Institutional demand faded, causing the Bitcoin rally to stall below $76,000.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Google DeepMind Releases Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6
items.summary: Google DeepMind shipped Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6, improving visual/spatial reasoning, safer physical reasoning, and tool use for robotics, with 93% success on instrument-reading tasks and availability via Gemini API and AI Studio.

items.title: OpenAI Launches GPT-5.4-Cyber for Defensive Security
items.summary: OpenAI expanded its Trusted Access for Cyber program with GPT-5.4-Cyber, a fine-tuned version of GPT-5.4 optimized for defensive security workflows, available to higher-tier authenticated defenders.

items.title: Hugging Face Introduces GPU Kernels on the Hub
items.summary: Hugging Face launched 'Kernels' as a new repo type on the Hub, offering precompiled GPU artifacts with 1.7x–2.5x speedups over PyTorch baselines for specific GPU/PyTorch/OS combinations.

items.title: Cursor Achieves 38% Speedup on CUDA Problems with Multi-Agent System
items.summary: Cursor developed a multi-agent CUDA optimization system with NVIDIA, delivering a 38% geomean speedup across 235 CUDA problems in three weeks, showcasing agent applications in systems optimization.

items.title: West Health-Gallup: 66 Million Americans Use AI for Healthcare Advice
items.summary: A new survey reveals one in four U.S. adults (over 66 million) have used AI tools for physical or mental healthcare information, often to supplement doctor visits, with motivations including quick answers and curiosity.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Amazon Acquires Globalstar for $11.57 Billion to Boost Satellite Internet and Challenge Starlink
items.summary: Amazon announced the acquisition of satellite operator Globalstar for $11.57 billion on April 14 to enhance its Project Kuiper (rebranded Amazon Leo) with low-Earth orbit satellites and direct-to-device capabilities. The deal includes a partnership with Apple for satellite connectivity in iPhones and Apple Watches.

items.title: Meta Extends Broadcom AI Chip Deal Through 2029 to Reduce Nvidia Dependency
items.summary: Meta expanded its custom chip partnership with Broadcom through 2029, committing to over one gigawatt of computing capacity to support AI across its platforms and build in-house silicon strategy.

items.title: HubSpot Launches Answer Engine Optimization Tools at Spring 2026 Spotlight
items.summary: HubSpot unveiled over 100 product updates, including Answer Engine Optimization (AEO) for tracking brand visibility in AI answer engines, Smart Deal Progression, and expanded Prospecting and Customer Agents.

items.title: Instacart Acquires Instaleap to Expand Enterprise Platform Internationally
items.summary: Instacart acquired Instaleap on April 14 to grow its enterprise platform beyond the U.S. market.

items.title: Exowatt Raises $140M for Solar Power Solution Targeting AI Data Center Energy Crisis
items.summary: Exowatt, backed by a16z and others, develops sand-based thermal storage using concentrated sunlight for 24/7 off-grid power, addressing data center power demands with deployments planned for 2025.
